title: श्री गणेश भुजङ्ग स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गणेश भुजङ्ग स्तोत्रम् ॥

रणत्क्षुद्रघण्टानिनादाभिरामं
चलत्ताण्डवोद्दण्डवत्पद्मताडम्।
मिलद्भृङ्गसङ्घोपगीतासमान-
प्रभुं गौरिनन्दं भजे वक्रतुण्डम्॥ १॥

प्रकाशत्सुवृत्तोत्तमाङ्गोज्ज्वलाङ्गं
विदर्भत्सुवृत्तातिमोदप्रवाहम्।
गजाक्ष्माभृदाकारमाशापदेशं
प्रभुं गौरिनन्दं भजे वक्रतुण्डम्॥ २॥

विचित्रस्फुरद्रत्नमालाकिरीटं
किरीटोल्लसच्चन्द्ररेखाविभूषम्।
विभूषैकभूषं भवध्वंसहेतुं
प्रभुं गौरिनन्दं भजे वक्रतुण्डम्॥ ३॥

उदञ्चद्भुजावल्लरीदृश्यमूलं
स्फुरच्छुण्डदण्डोल्लसद्बीजपूरम्।
गलद्दर्पसौगन्ध्यलोलालिमालं
प्रभुं गौरिनन्दं भजे वक्रतुण्डम्॥ ४॥

प्रलम्बोदरं वारणेन्द्रानुकारं
कराम्भोजपद्मप्रभापूरहस्तम्।
कलाचन्द्रचूडं त्रिनेत्रप्रसन्नं
प्रभुं गौरिनन्दं भजे वक्रतुण्डम्॥ ५॥

विचित्रं तमोमूषिकारूढमेकं
त्रिलोकैकनाथं विभुं विश्वनाथम्।
मखाग्रेसमाराध्यमानं शिवांशं
प्रभुं गौरिनन्दं भजे वक्रतुण्डम्॥ ६॥

महागणपत्यष्टकं भक्तिपूर्वं
पठेद्यस्तु तस्य प्रसीदेत्स विघ्नेशः।
नरस्तस्य विद्यार्थसिद्धिर्भवेच्च
समृद्धिर्यशो धीर्बलं चाऽऽयुरारोग्यम्॥ ७॥

॥ इति श्रीमच्छंकराचार्यविरचितम् श्री गणेश भुजङ्ग स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ganesh Bhujanga Stotram ||

Ranat-kshudra-ghanta-ninada-abhiramam
Chalat-tandava-uddanda-vat-padma-tadam |
Milad-bhringa-sangha-upagita-asamana-
Prabhum Gauri-nandam bhaje Vakratundam || 1 ||

Prakashat-suvritta-uttama-anga-ujjvala-angam
Vidarbhat-suvritta-ati-moda-pravaham |
Gaja-akshma-bhrid-akaram-asha-padesham
Prabhum Gauri-nandam bhaje Vakratundam || 2 ||

Vichitra-sphurat-ratna-mala-kiritam
Kirita-ullasach-chandra-rekha-vibhusham |
Vibhushaika-bhusham bhava-dhvamsa-hetum
Prabhum Gauri-nandam bhaje Vakratundam || 3 ||

Udanchad-bhuja-avallari-drishya-mulam
Sphurach-chunda-danda-ullasad-bija-puram |
Galad-darpa-saugandhya-lola-ali-malam
Prabhum Gauri-nandam bhaje Vakratundam || 4 ||

Pralamba-udaram varana-indra-anukaram
Kara-ambhoja-padma-prabha-pura-hastam |
Kala-chandra-chudam tri-netra-prasannam
Prabhum Gauri-nandam bhaje Vakratundam || 5 ||

Vichitram tamo-mushika-arudham-ekam
Tri-loka-eka-natham vibhum Vishvanatham |
Makha-agre-sama-aradhya-manam Shiva-amsham
Prabhum Gauri-nandam bhaje Vakratundam || 6 ||

Maha-Ganapaty-ashtakam bhakti-purvam
Pathed-yas-tu tasya prasidет sa Vighneshah |
Naras-tasya vidya-artha-siddhir-bhavech-cha
Samriddhir-yasho dhir-balam cha-ayur-arogyam || 7 ||

|| Iti Shrimach-Shankaracharya-virachitam Shri Ganesh Bhujanga Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
घुँघरुओं की मधुर ध्वनि से मनोहर, ताण्डव में उद्दण्ड पद्मताड़न करते, भ्रमरों के समूह द्वारा गीत-सा गाये जाते — ऐसे गौरीनन्दन वक्रतुण्ड प्रभु को भजता हूँ॥ १॥

प्रकाशमान गोल उत्तम अंगों से उज्ज्वल, अति मोदप्रवाह वाले, गजराज-सा आकार, दिशाओं के स्वामी — गौरीनन्दन वक्रतुण्ड को भजता हूँ॥ २॥

विचित्र रत्नमाला-किरीट से शोभित, किरीट पर चन्द्ररेखा विभूषित, भवबन्धन विध्वंस के कारण — गौरीनन्दन वक्रतुण्ड को भजता हूँ॥ ३॥

ऊँची भुजाओं की लता से शोभित मूल वाले, शुण्ड-दण्ड पर बीजपूर (नींबू) चमकाते, सुगन्ध से लोलुप भ्रमरमाला वाले — गौरीनन्दन को भजता हूँ॥ ४॥

लम्बोदर, गजराज-सा, कमल-हस्त, कलाचन्द्र चूड़, त्रिनेत्र प्रसन्न — गौरीनन्दन को भजता हूँ॥ ५॥

मूषक-वाहन, त्रिलोकनाथ, विश्वनाथ, यज्ञ में सर्वप्रथम पूजित, शिवांश — गौरीनन्दन वक्रतुण्ड को भजता हूँ॥ ६॥

जो भक्तिपूर्वक इस स्तोत्र को पढ़े, उस पर विघ्नेश प्रसन्न होते हैं — विद्या, अर्थ-सिद्धि, समृद्धि, यश, बुद्धि, बल, आयु और आरोग्य प्राप्त होता है॥ ७॥
benefits: Composed in elegant Bhujanga metre by Adi Shankaracharya
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of obstacles and grants success in endeavors
Invokes the blessing of vidya (knowledge), wealth, fame, and long life
Ideal for chanting before starting any new venture
Invokes the grace of vighneshwara — the lord of obstacles
Beautiful poetic description of Ganesha's divine form
description: Ganesh Bhujanga Stotram — श्री गणेश भुजङ्ग स्तोत्रम् — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Lord Ganesha. This revered text originates from Ganesh Bhujanga Stotram — Adi Shankaracharya. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to beautiful poetic description of Ganesha's divine form as well as grant the grace of Vighneshwara — the lord of obstacles. Additionally, it is known to remove all obstacles and grant success in endeavors. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Ganesh Chaturthi and Sankashti Chaturthi. Comprising 7 verses, it can be completed in approximately 5 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Ganesh Bhujanga Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Ganesh Bhujanga Stotram by Adi Shankaracharya in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Serpentine hymn to Lord Ganesha for obstacle removal.
relatedSearches: Ganesha Bhujangam meaning verse by verse
Vak siddhi blessing from Ganesha stotram

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Ganesha Bhujanga Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ganesha Bhujanga Stotram is traditionally attributed to Adi Shankaracharya (788–820 CE), the great Advaita Vedanta master who is also credited with parallel Bhujangam stotras to Shiva (Shiva Bhujangam) and Subrahmanya (Subrahmanya Bhujangam). The composition belongs to a coordinated group of stotras Shankara composed in the bhujanga-prayata meter for principal Hindu deities, integrating Vedantic non-dualism with classical bhakti devotion. The Ganesha Bhujangam focuses specifically on the Nritya-Ganapati form — Ganesha as the cosmic dancer — and is one of his most lyrical Ganesha hymns. (Sources: Hinduism Outlook; AstroVed; Stotram.co.in.)

seoFaqs.question: What does 'Bhujanga' mean in the title?
seoFaqs.answer: Bhujanga literally means 'serpent' in Sanskrit, and refers here not to the deity but to the bhujanga-prayata chandas (meter), so named because its rhythmic flow resembles the gliding movement of a snake. The meter consists of four padas of twelve syllables each, in a yagana-yagana-yagana-yagana pattern (laghu-guru-guru repeated) producing a fluid, hypnotic cadence ideal for singing. The serpentine flow makes it especially suited to dance-stotras such as the Nritya-Ganapati Bhujangam, where the meter mirrors the deity's flowing dance movements. The same meter is used in Subrahmanya Bhujangam and Shiva Bhujangam, also by Adi Shankaracharya. (Sources: Hinduism Outlook; Devshoppe; Sanskritdocuments.)

seoFaqs.question: Which form of Ganesha does the stotram celebrate?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ganesha Bhujangam focuses primarily on Nritya Ganapati — the dancing form of Ganesha — describing him with luminous attributes such as a complexion of red coral, japa-kusuma flower brilliance, the radiance of the rising sun, a large protruding belly (lambodara), a curved trunk and a single tusk. The dance-form imagery emphasises Ganesha as the cosmic remover of obstacles in motion, joyfully expressing the rhythm of the universe. Many of the verses also salute his role as the lord of the ganas, the son of Shiva and Parvati, and the foremost deity to be invoked at the beginning of any auspicious undertaking. (Sources: Hinduism Outlook; AstroVed; Bhakti Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: How long is the stotram and what is its structure?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ganesha Bhujangam typically contains nine to thirteen verses (the count varies slightly by manuscript, with the most common versions printing nine main shlokas plus a phalashruti). Each verse is in the bhujanga-prayata meter and addresses a specific aspect of Ganesha's iconography or function — the dancing form, the elephant face, the single tusk, the modaka-bearing hand, the role as Vighnaharta, the patronage of vidya, the relationship with Shiva and Parvati, and the protective grace bestowed on devotees. The phalashruti at the close describes the benefits of regular recitation. The full stotra takes about four to six minutes at a measured chant. (Sources: Vaidika Vignanam; Stotram.co.in; Hindu Gallery.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the phalashruti promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The concluding phalashruti states that those who study or recite the stotram with devotion in the morning, having woken up and bathed, attain all desired material objects (sarvarthan), gain Vak-siddhi (the power by which spoken wishes are fulfilled), achieve victory over enemies and obstacles, become beloved of Lakshmi, and ultimately reach the lotus feet of Ganesha. The Hinduism Outlook commentary specifically mentions removal of obstacles, growth in intellect and creative pursuits (apt for students, artists and scholars), and spiritual elevation through Ganesha's grace. (Sources: AstroVed; Hinduism Outlook; Vaidika Vignanam.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Ganesha Bhujangam differ from the Ganesha Pancharatnam?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are Adi Shankaracharya compositions to Ganesha, but they differ in meter, length and emphasis. Ganesha Pancharatnam ('the five jewels of Ganesha') is a five-verse hymn in the panchachamara meter, structured as five gem-like meditations on Ganesha as the sole refuge — its tone is contemplative and Vedantic. The Ganesha Bhujangam is longer (nine to thirteen verses), set to the flowing bhujanga-prayata meter, and describes Ganesha primarily through the iconography of the dancing form, with greater emphasis on the lyrical bhakti dimension. Pancharatnam is recited as a daily japa-stotra; Bhujangam is preferred for festival occasions and Nritya-Ganapati worship. (Sources: Stotram.co.in; Sanskritdocuments; Hinduism Outlook.)
